content: agent2 here: implementing working_dir param for click.edit() - will touch src/click/termui.py and src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class). Will coordinate if overlap.

ack. I'll add working_dir to Editor.__init__ (new kwarg), edit_file (subprocess cwd=self.working_dir), and click.edit() in termui.py. I'll keep edits minimal/additive to reduce conflicts with your escape_shell work. Will re-read file before each edit to catch your changes. Ping me before you touch edit_file's subprocess.Popen call so we don't clobber.

Heads up: I don't see escape_shell in the current file yet (Editor.__init__ still only has working_dir, edit_file's Popen still just has cwd=self.working_dir, no shlex/escape_shell logic). Possibly your edit failed silently (maybe old_string mismatch since I'd already edited that block first). Current edit_file body:
    def edit_file(self, filename: str) -> None:
        import subprocess
        editor = self.get_editor()
        environ = None
        if self.env:
            environ = os.environ.copy()
            environ.update(self.env)
        try:
            c = subprocess.Popen(
                f'{editor} "{filename}"',
                env=environ,
                shell=True,
                cwd=self.working_dir,
            )
            ...
Please re-apply your escape_shell logic on top of this (just add your quoting logic before the Popen call, keep cwd=self.working_dir in the Popen kwargs). Re-read the file first to get latest state.

Note: git diff --stat right now only shows CHANGES.rst, src/click/_termui_impl.py, src/click/termui.py with just my working_dir changes (9 lines added in _termui_impl.py, 15 in termui.py) - your escape_shell edit doesn't appear to have landed in the shared working tree yet. Since we share the same repo dir, whoever writes patch.txt last via 'git diff' will capture BOTH our changes combined. To keep our submissions clean/independent, I'm going to build my patch.txt by reconstructing only my own edits against the original HEAD content (not a raw 'git diff'), so it won't include your escape_shell changes even if they land later. You should probably do the same for yours. Let me know when your escape_shell change is actually applied so I can double check my reconstruction excludes it correctly.

Done on my end: patch.txt written to /workspace/repo/patch.txt containing only my working_dir changes (reconstructed against original HEAD, so it's clean regardless of your escape_shell edits in the shared working tree). Verified it applies cleanly to a fresh checkout and tests pass. Go ahead and do the same for your feature when ready - happy to double check if useful.
